 {"id": "flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.6", "title": "[T1-MSG.2] fcp.teams — Microsoft Teams Bot + Graph API Connector", "description": "Microsoft Teams connector via Bot Framework + Graph API. 320M+ monthly users, dominant enterprise messaging platform. Operations: teams.send_message (channel/chat), teams.send_card (adaptive cards), teams.reply_message, teams.list_channels, teams.list_members, teams.create_channel, teams.get_message, teams.search_messages. Auth: Azure AD OAuth2 with bot registration. Webhook: Bot Framework activity handler for incoming messages/events. Network: graph.microsoft.com + login.microsoftonline.com. Archetype: Bidirectional. Requires Azure AD app registration with Teams bot permissions. 4 subtasks: types/client/error, connector/manifest, webhook/activity handler, tests.", "status": "open", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "feature", "created_at": "2026-03-17T21:54:50.496331Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "updated_at": "2026-03-18T00:25:35.132813Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.6", "depends_on_id": "flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1", "type": "parent-child", "created_at": "2026-03-17T21:54:50.496331Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}], "comments": [{"id": 1783, "issue_id": "flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.6", "author": "jemanuel", "text": "Testing requirements (per project standard):

UNIT TESTS (50+ minimum, wiremock-based):
- All operations: success path, error paths (auth fail, rate limit, not found, server error)
- Client retry: 429 with retry-after, 500 backoff, cancellation during sleep
- Error mapping: every error variant → FcpError, is_retryable classification
- Config parsing: all auth modes, validation (both/neither auth), URL trim
- Webhook/event parsing: all message/event types, malformed payloads
- Serialization: request/response roundtrips

INTEGRATION TESTS (10+ minimum):
- ConnectorSuite: introspection returns correct operation count
- ComplianceSuite: default-deny for unconfigured operations  
- Full operation cycle: configure → invoke → response validation
- Rate limit exhaustion and recovery
- Shutdown: runtime.shutdown() propagates to client
- E2E compliance script passes

STRUCTURED LOGGING:
- Every operation emits tracing spans with connector_id, operation_id, duration_ms
- Retry events emit attempt, delay_ms, error fields
- All logs machine-parseable JSON", "created_at": "2026-03-18T00:24:49Z"}, {"id": 1818, "issue_id": "flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.6", "author": "jemanuel", "text": "TEAMS-SPECIFIC TESTING:
- Bot Framework activity handler: verify adaptive card rendering
- Azure AD token refresh: test expired token auto-refresh
- Tenant isolation: verify multi-tenant deployment safety
- @mention parsing: test bot mention extraction from message text
- Channel vs. direct message routing: test both paths
- Teams webhook signature verification (HMAC-SHA256)", "created_at": "2026-03-18T00:25:35Z"}]}
 {"id": "flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.6.1", "title": "Teams: tenant topology, app registration, and platform scope", "description": "Front-load the Teams-specific platform scoping that sits between the generic V3 contract and the concrete implementation tasks: tenant topology, bot registration model, channel/install prerequisites, conversation identity boundaries, and which Graph surfaces are truly in the first slice. This task exists because Teams is more platform-shaped than a simple chat API and future contributors should not have to reconstruct those deployment assumptions from scratch.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-03-18T00:41:11.206840Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "updated_at": "2026-03-18T07:05:14.680575Z", "closed_at": "2026-03-18T07:05:14.680148Z", "close_reason": "done", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.6.1", "depends_on_id": "flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.6", "type": "parent-child", "created_at": "2026-03-18T00:41:11.206840Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}], "comments": [{"id": 1852, "issue_id": "flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.6.1", "author": "jemanuel", "text": "Teams platform scoping:

**Tenant topology**: Teams operates within Microsoft 365 tenants. Each tenant is an isolated Azure AD directory. A Teams app (bot) registers in a single tenant or as a multi-tenant app. For FCP, single-tenant registration is the default scope (admin-controlled, no marketplace listing).

**App registration model**: Teams bots use Azure Bot Service, registered in Azure AD. The bot has: Application (client) ID, Client Secret (or certificate), Tenant ID, and a Bot Framework messaging endpoint. Registration via Azure Portal > App Registrations > New Registration > Add Bot Channel.

**Channel topology**:
- Team -> Channels (standard, private, shared)
- Group chats (up to 250 members)  
- 1:1 chats
- Meeting chats (ephemeral, tied to meeting lifecycle)
Bot receives activities via the Bot Framework SDK's messaging endpoint.

**Conversation identity boundaries**:
- conversationId uniquely identifies a chat/channel context
- turnContext.activity contains sender, channel, team, tenant info
- Bot can only respond in conversations where it's installed
- Channel vs. personal scope determined by installation context

**Graph API surfaces (first slice)**:
- Chat messages: GET /teams/{id}/channels/{id}/messages (delegated or application)
- Channel messages: POST to send, PATCH to update
- Teams/channels: list, get (read-only in first slice)
- Users/members: list members of teams/channels
- Proactive messaging: requires serviceUrl + conversationId from prior activity

**Prerequisites**:
- Azure AD tenant with Teams license
- Azure Bot Service registration
- App manifest (teams-app-manifest.json) with bot section
- Admin consent for application permissions (optional; user consent for delegated)

**Non-goals (first slice)**: No Adaptive Cards authoring, no meeting integration, no Tabs/Task modules, no SharePoint integration, no Power Platform connectors, no Marketplace listing.", "created_at": "2026-03-18T07:04:47Z"}]}
 {"id": "flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.6.2", "title": "Teams: V3 contract, auth boundary, operation inventory, and safety matrix", "description": "Define the V3 contract for Teams before implementation expands. Pin down the auth boundary, configuration schema, operation inventory,  safety/risk/idempotency classifications, explicit non-goals, and how this connector maps onto the campaign-wide V3 acceptance contract. Capture the split between Bot Framework activity handling and Microsoft Graph resource access, including tenant/app registration, webhook ingress expectations, and the boundary between chat actions, files, calendar data, and administrative operations.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-03-18T00:42:09.665348Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "updated_at": "2026-03-18T07:22:27.384980Z", "closed_at": "2026-03-18T07:22:27.384489Z", "close_reason": "done", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.6.2", "depends_on_id": "flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.6", "type": "parent-child", "created_at": "2026-03-18T00:42:09.665348Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.6.2", "depends_on_id": "flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.6.1", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-18T00:51:02.057712Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.6.2", "depends_on_id": "flywheel_connectors-j05nu.11.1", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-18T00:43:33.792445Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.6.2", "depends_on_id": "flywheel_connectors-j05nu.11.3", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-18T00:43:47.887646Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}]}

Cover OAuth/service-principal token acquisition, conversation/thread identifiers, attachment metadata, paging, throttling, and Graph/Bot error normalization.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-03-18T00:42:38.824555Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "updated_at": "2026-03-19T02:40:56.021203+00:00", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.6.3", "depends_on_id": "flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.6", "type": "parent-child", "created_at": "2026-03-18T00:42:38.824555Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.6.3", "depends_on_id": "flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.6.2", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-18T00:44:03.050071Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.6.3", "depends_on_id": "flywheel_connectors-j05nu.11.2", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-18T00:44:19.905659Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.6.3", "depends_on_id": "flywheel_connectors-j05nu.9.1", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-18T00:44:34.592237Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}], "closed_at": "2026-03-19T02:40:56.020999+00:00", "closed_reason": "done"}

 {"id": "flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.7", "title": "[T1-MSG.3] fcp.signal — Signal Messenger Connector (via signal-cli bridge)", "description": "# Signal Connector (via `signal-cli` bridge)

## Why this belongs in Tier 1
Signal is one of the most important privacy-first messaging gaps in the current connector inventory. It does not have the raw global reach of WhatsApp, but it is disproportionately important for security-sensitive teams and users who explicitly avoid cloud-hosted chat platforms.

## API surface and operating model
This connector targets a self-hosted `signal-cli` bridge rather than a first-party bot API. That choice has architectural consequences that must stay visible in the implementation plan:
- outbound send operations are bridge-mediated request/response calls,
- inbound messages and group events are bridge-backed polling or event delivery flows,
- account registration and trust state are external prerequisites,
- readiness and doctor outputs must explain bridge health instead of pretending Signal is a plain hosted API.

## Expected operations
- `signal.messages.send_text|send_attachment`
- `signal.messages.receive|list`
- `signal.contacts.list|get`
- `signal.groups.list|get`
- `signal.identities.trust`
- `signal.profile.get`

## V3 implementation shape
This feature is intentionally broken into a five-step V3 ladder:
1. define the connector contract, auth boundary, bridge topology, safety matrix, and first-slice non-goals;
2. build typed config, client, state, and error mapping for the bridge boundary;
3. wire the V3 runtime, manifest, handlers, and introspection surfaces;
4. implement receive loops, group/message state handling, and attachment flows;
5. close with readiness, remediation guidance, privacy-safe observability, and explicit conformance evidence.

## Critical considerations
- The manifest must declare the bridge prerequisite truthfully.
- Logging and doctor output must never leak sensitive identifiers or message content.
- The first safe slice should stay conservative about identity/trust mutation semantics.
- The verification bead must prove the bridge-backed architecture is operable, not just compilable.", "status": "open", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "feature", "created_at": "2026-03-17T21:55:03.215435Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "updated_at": "2026-03-18T01:37:09.516287Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.7", "depends_on_id": "flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1", "type": "parent-child", "created_at": "2026-03-17T21:55:03.215435Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}], "comments": [{"id": 1784, "issue_id": "flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.7", "author": "jemanuel", "text": "Testing requirements (per project standard):

UNIT TESTS (50+ minimum, wiremock-based):
- All operations: success path, error paths (auth fail, rate limit, not found, server error)
- Client retry: 429 with retry-after, 500 backoff, cancellation during sleep
- Error mapping: every error variant → FcpError, is_retryable classification
- Config parsing: all auth modes, validation (both/neither auth), URL trim
- Webhook/event parsing: all message/event types, malformed payloads
- Serialization: request/response roundtrips

INTEGRATION TESTS (10+ minimum):
- ConnectorSuite: introspection returns correct operation count
- ComplianceSuite: default-deny for unconfigured operations  
- Full operation cycle: configure → invoke → response validation
- Rate limit exhaustion and recovery
- Shutdown: runtime.shutdown() propagates to client
- E2E compliance script passes

STRUCTURED LOGGING:
- Every operation emits tracing spans with connector_id, operation_id, duration_ms
- Retry events emit attempt, delay_ms, error fields
- All logs machine-parseable JSON", "created_at": "2026-03-18T00:24:49Z"}, {"id": 1819, "issue_id": "flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.7", "author": "jemanuel", "text": "SIGNAL-SPECIFIC TESTING:
- Bridge availability check: test graceful failure when bridge is down
- Bridge version compatibility: verify minimum signal-cli version
- Phone number format validation: E.164 format enforcement
- Group message handling: test group send/receive
- Attachment size limits: test file upload constraints
- Trust/safety key verification: test identity trust flow
- Polling interval: test configurable poll frequency
- Reconnection: test bridge restart recovery", "created_at": "2026-03-18T00:25:35Z"}]}
 {"id": "flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.7.1", "title": "Signal: V3 contract, auth boundary, operation inventory, and safety matrix", "description": "Define the V3 contract for Signal before implementation expands. Pin down the auth boundary, configuration schema, operation inventory,  safety/risk/idempotency classifications, explicit non-goals, and how this connector maps onto the campaign-wide V3 acceptance contract. Define the supported  bridge topology, account-registration assumptions, daemon health requirements, and which messaging, group, attachment, and profile operations are in scope versus deferred.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-03-18T00:46:58.209777Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "updated_at": "2026-03-18T06:18:29.507523Z", "closed_at": "2026-03-18T06:18:29.507136Z", "close_reason": "done", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.7.1", "depends_on_id": "flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.7", "type": "parent-child", "created_at": "2026-03-18T00:46:58.209777Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.7.1", "depends_on_id": "flywheel_connectors-j05nu.11.1", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-18T00:48:06.673286Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.7.1", "depends_on_id": "flywheel_connectors-j05nu.11.3", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-18T00:48:20.224717Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}], "comments": [{"id": 1833, "issue_id": "flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.7.1", "author": "jemanuel", "text": "V3 contract for Signal (signal-cli bridge):

**Archetype**: Bridge/Daemon + Bidirectional (unique: requires external bridge process)
**Auth model**: signal-cli daemon must be running with registered phone number. Connector communicates via signal-cli JSON-RPC HTTP API (localhost). No direct Signal Protocol implementation — all crypto delegated to signal-cli.
**Network**: localhost only (bridge HTTP API). deny_private=true, deny_localhost=false.

**Bridge prerequisites**:
- signal-cli >= 0.13.0 installed and in PATH
- Phone number registered: signal-cli -u +NUMBER register / verify
- Daemon mode: signal-cli -u +NUMBER daemon --http --receive-mode=on-connection
- Bridge health endpoint: GET http://localhost:8080/v1/about

**Operations (first slice)**:
| Operation | SafetyTier | RiskLevel | IdempotencyClass | Capability |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| signal.send_message | Risky | Medium | BestEffort | signal.send |
| signal.send_reaction | Risky | Low | BestEffort | signal.send |
| signal.receive | Safe | Low | None | signal.read |
| signal.list_contacts | Safe | Low | None | signal.read |
| signal.list_groups | Safe | Low | None | signal.read |
| signal.get_group | Safe | Low | None | signal.read |
| signal.update_profile | Risky | Low | BestEffort | signal.admin |
| signal.delete_message | Dangerous | High | Strict | signal.send |
| signal.health | Safe | Low | Strict | signal.read |

**Safety policy**: End-to-end encryption handled by signal-cli. Connector never sees plaintext keys. Delete message is Dangerous (irreversible from sender perspective). Group creation/management deferred to future slice.

**Provisioning**: Manual phone registration via signal-cli CLI. Connector's doctor checks bridge availability, version, and registration status.

**Non-goals (first slice)**: No group creation/administration, no file attachments > 100MB, no Signal Desktop linking, no direct libsignal integration.", "created_at": "2026-03-18T06:18:21Z"}]}

 {"id": "flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.8", "title": "[T1-MSG.4] fcp.matrix — Matrix Federated Messaging Connector", "description": "# Matrix Federated Messaging Connector

## Why this belongs in Tier 1
Matrix is the highest-value open and federated messaging addition in the campaign. It complements proprietary chat platforms by giving FCP a protocol-native path into self-hosted and federation-aware deployments.

## API surface and workflow role
The connector targets the Matrix Client-Server API and therefore needs to handle both read-heavy synchronization and write-capable room workflows:
- room and membership discovery,
- message and media send flows,
- sync-loop event ingestion,
- room state and membership transitions,
- configurable homeserver deployment assumptions.

## Expected operations
- `matrix.messages.send|get|list`
- `matrix.media.send|get`
- `matrix.rooms.list|get|join|leave`
- `matrix.members.list|invite`
- `matrix.sync.run`
- `matrix.rooms.set_topic`

## V3 implementation shape
This feature is intentionally planned as a five-step ladder:
1. define homeserver assumptions, auth boundary, `OperationInfo` safety semantics, and first-slice E2EE non-goals;
2. build typed client/config/error/state foundations;
3. wire runtime, manifest, operation handlers, and truthful introspection;
4. implement sync-loop state, membership, media, and event translation flows;
5. finish with readiness, federation-aware remediation guidance, observability, and conformance evidence.

## Critical considerations
- The first slice must be explicit about what is not yet supported around E2EE.
- Self-hosted homeserver configuration and federation caveats must appear in readiness and manifest surfaces.
- The sync loop is a core behavioral surface, not a side detail.
- Verification must show both safe read flows and mutation-heavy room workflows behave predictably under V3 semantics.", "status": "open", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "feature", "created_at": "2026-03-17T21:55:15.820830Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "updated_at": "2026-03-18T01:37:09.733755Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.8", "depends_on_id": "flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1", "type": "parent-child", "created_at": "2026-03-17T21:55:15.820830Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}], "comments": [{"id": 1785, "issue_id": "flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.8", "author": "jemanuel", "text": "Testing requirements (per project standard):

UNIT TESTS (50+ minimum, wiremock-based):
- All operations: success path, error paths (auth fail, rate limit, not found, server error)
- Client retry: 429 with retry-after, 500 backoff, cancellation during sleep
- Error mapping: every error variant → FcpError, is_retryable classification
- Config parsing: all auth modes, validation (both/neither auth), URL trim
- Webhook/event parsing: all message/event types, malformed payloads
- Serialization: request/response roundtrips

INTEGRATION TESTS (10+ minimum):
- ConnectorSuite: introspection returns correct operation count
- ComplianceSuite: default-deny for unconfigured operations  
- Full operation cycle: configure → invoke → response validation
- Rate limit exhaustion and recovery
- Shutdown: runtime.shutdown() propagates to client
- E2E compliance script passes

STRUCTURED LOGGING:
- Every operation emits tracing spans with connector_id, operation_id, duration_ms
- Retry events emit attempt, delay_ms, error fields
- All logs machine-parseable JSON", "created_at": "2026-03-18T00:24:50Z"}]}
 {"id": "flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.8.1", "title": "Matrix: V3 contract, auth boundary, operation inventory, and safety matrix", "description": "Define the V3 contract for Matrix before implementation expands. Pin down homeserver configuration, auth boundary, room or thread or message operation inventory, OperationInfo safety and risk semantics, media scope, membership changes, and explicit E2EE non-goals for the first safe slice.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-03-18T00:54:15.477529Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "updated_at": "2026-03-18T06:20:03.785057Z", "closed_at": "2026-03-18T06:20:03.784640Z", "close_reason": "done", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.8.1", "depends_on_id": "flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.8", "type": "parent-child", "created_at": "2026-03-18T00:54:15.477529Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.8.1", "depends_on_id": "flywheel_connectors-j05nu.11.1", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-18T00:54:15.477529Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.8.1", "depends_on_id": "flywheel_connectors-j05nu.11.3", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-18T00:54:15.477529Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}], "comments": [{"id": 1834, "issue_id": "flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.8.1", "author": "jemanuel", "text": "V3 contract for Matrix (Client-Server API):

**Archetype**: Bidirectional + Streaming (federated protocol, self-hosted or hosted)
**Auth model**: Matrix access_token from homeserver login. Supports: password login, SSO/OIDC, or pre-provisioned access token. Homeserver URL configurable (default: matrix.org).
**Network**: Homeserver URL (configurable). TLS required for non-localhost. Federation endpoints NOT accessed (client-server API only).

**Operations (first slice)**:
| Operation | SafetyTier | RiskLevel | IdempotencyClass | Capability |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| matrix.send_message | Risky | Medium | Strict | matrix.send |
| matrix.send_reaction | Risky | Low | Strict | matrix.send |
| matrix.redact_event | Dangerous | High | Strict | matrix.send |
| matrix.join_room | Risky | Medium | Strict | matrix.rooms |
| matrix.leave_room | Risky | Medium | Strict | matrix.rooms |
| matrix.list_rooms | Safe | Low | None | matrix.read |
| matrix.get_room_messages | Safe | Low | None | matrix.read |
| matrix.sync | Safe | Low | None | matrix.read |
| matrix.get_profile | Safe | Low | None | matrix.read |
| matrix.set_display_name | Risky | Low | BestEffort | matrix.admin |
| matrix.upload_media | Risky | Medium | BestEffort | matrix.send |
| matrix.health | Safe | Low | Strict | matrix.read |

**Streaming**: Long-poll /sync endpoint with since token. Cursor state (since token) externalized. Reconnection with exponential backoff.

**Safety policy**: Event redaction is Dangerous (permanently removes content from federation timeline). Room join/leave affects federation state. Transaction IDs used for idempotency on send operations (Matrix spec txnId).

**Provisioning**: Login to homeserver with credentials. Doctor checks: homeserver reachable, access_token valid, user profile accessible.

**Non-goals (first slice)**: No room creation/administration, no E2EE (Olm/Megolm key management), no federation server-to-server API, no space hierarchy, no VoIP/call signaling.", "created_at": "2026-03-18T06:19:16Z"}]}

 {"id": "flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.8.5", "title": "Matrix: readiness, observability, docs, compliance, and verification", "description": "Finish Matrix with operator-facing readiness, doctor remediation guidance, sync-loop observability, deterministic federation-safe tests, and explicit conformance evidence for both read-heavy and mutation-heavy room workflows.", "status": "open", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-03-18T00:55:10.811005Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "updated_at": "2026-03-18T01:45:28.238476Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.8.5", "depends_on_id": "flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.8", "type": "parent-child", "created_at": "2026-03-18T00:55:10.811005Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.8.5", "depends_on_id": "flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.8.4", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-18T00:55:10.811005Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.8.5", "depends_on_id": "flywheel_connectors-j05nu.11.4", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-18T01:45:28.237803Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.8.5", "depends_on_id": "flywheel_connectors-j05nu.9.2", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-18T01:44:53.617500Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.8.5", "depends_on_id": "flywheel_connectors-j05nu.9.3", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-18T01:45:10.728404Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.8.5", "depends_on_id": "flywheel_connectors-j05nu.9.4", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-18T00:55:10.811005Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}]}
 {"id": "flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.9", "title": "[T1-MSG.5] fcp.imessage — iMessage Connector (macOS BlueBubbles bridge)", "description": "# iMessage Connector (BlueBubbles bridge)

## Why this belongs in Tier 1
This connector gives the campaign an Apple ecosystem path without pretending Apple provides a normal bot platform. The realistic route is the BlueBubbles bridge, which exposes a stable HTTP/event surface over a macOS host that is already running Messages.app.

## API surface and operating model
This is a bridge-backed connector with strong local-privacy implications:
- outbound message and media sends are proxied through BlueBubbles,
- inbound message/chat updates come from bridge event delivery or polling,
- a macOS host is an explicit prerequisite,
- doctor/readiness output must surface host and bridge health truthfully.

## Expected operations
- `imessage.messages.send_text|send_attachment|get|search`
- `imessage.chats.list|get`
- `imessage.messages.mark_read`
- `imessage.sync.events`

## V3 implementation shape
The feature is deliberately structured as a five-step ladder:
1. define macOS/bridge prerequisites, auth boundary, safe first-slice operation inventory, and non-goals;
2. build typed config, bridge client, error mapping, and chat/message state foundations;
3. wire runtime, manifest, handlers, and introspection around a bridge-backed connector;
4. implement event delivery, chat/thread mapping, and media synchronization behavior;
5. close with operator guidance, bridge-health doctor checks, privacy-safe observability, and conformance evidence.

## Critical considerations
- The manifest must tell the truth about macOS and BlueBubbles prerequisites.
- Local/private deployment must not become an excuse for weak safety modeling.
- Logging and diagnostics must stay careful around message content and identifiers.
- Verification must prove bridge operability and remediation clarity, not just API happy paths.", "status": "open", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "feature", "created_at": "2026-03-17T21:55:28.658665Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "updated_at": "2026-03-18T01:37:09.943921Z", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.9", "depends_on_id": "flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1", "type": "parent-child", "created_at": "2026-03-17T21:55:28.658665Z", "created_by": "jemanuel", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}], "comments": [{"id": 1786, "issue_id": "flywheel_connectors-j05nu.1.9", "author": "jemanuel", "text": "Testing requirements (per project standard):

UNIT TESTS (50+ minimum, wiremock-based):
- All operations: success path, error paths (auth fail, rate limit, not found, server error)
- Client retry: 429 with retry-after, 500 backoff, cancellation during sleep
- Error mapping: every error variant → FcpError, is_retryable classification
- Config parsing: all auth modes, validation (both/neither auth), URL trim
- Webhook/event parsing: all message/event types, malformed payloads
- Serialization: request/response roundtrips

INTEGRATION TESTS (10+ minimum):
- ConnectorSuite: introspection returns correct operation count
- ComplianceSuite: default-deny for unconfigured operations  
- Full operation cycle: configure → invoke → response validation
- Rate limit exhaustion and recovery
- Shutdown: runtime.shutdown() propagates to client
- E2E compliance script passes

STRUCTURED LOGGING:
- Every operation emits tracing spans with connector_id, operation_id, duration_ms
- Retry events emit attempt, delay_ms, error fields
- All logs machine-parseable JSON", "created_at": "2026-03-18T00:24:50Z"}]}
